mechanism_narrative: A downward move in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier liquidity conditions, lifting equity valuations and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because easing then reads as support rather than panic.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after declining FEDFUNDS than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and rising recession odds, which should push S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when payroll growth is also softening.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if negative returns fail to concentrate when UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue support for large-cap equities, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over roughly one quarter. The signal fails when the sales increase is driven by inflation alone rather than real demand.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS does not predict higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship vanishes after controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which improves cyclicals and aggregate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The pattern should fail when output rises only because of inventory rebuilding without demand follow-through.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not higher after positive INDPRO momentum, or if the effect disappears when HOUST is contracting sharply.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound signaled by HOUST moving above its recent baseline improves construction activity, household wealth effects, and broad cyclical sentiment, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is weaker when labor conditions are deteriorating at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-breakout episodes do not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect persists equally when UNRATE is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold, equity multiples compress and 20-day S&P 500 returns turn negative because discount-rate pressure dominates growth support. The signal should weaken once inflation is falling quickly enough to justify imminent policy cuts.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS periods do not show lower 5-day SPX returns than lower-rate periods, or if the sign remains negative even when CPIAUCSL inflation is sharply decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals broad labor-market weakening, reducing earnings visibility and raising recession risk, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. Either labor series alone is insufficient; the combination is required for the strongest signal.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase do not outperform either single series in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not more negative than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad real-demand expansion rather than a narrow nominal sales spike, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when GDP growth is revised away or retail sales growth is inflation-driven.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS strength do not deliver higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the relationship disappears after inflation-adjusting RSAFS with CPIAUCSL.
